compiled_state.title: Agents use code-reviewer-light (haiku) for merges and large batches — haiku lacks context for complex multi-file reviews
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## What happened

In host projects, the code-reviewer-light (haiku) agent is dispatched for merge commits and large batch commits. The haiku model lacks sufficient context to handle multi-file reviews properly — it produces false positives, misses cross-file interactions, and cannot reason about the relationships between changes across the batch.

## Expected behavior

Merge commits and large batch commits should NEVER be routed to the light tier (haiku). These changes inherently involve multiple files and cross-cutting concerns that require at least standard-tier (sonnet) review. The complexity classifier should enforce a minimum tier floor for:
1. Merge commits (detected via MERGE_HEAD)
2. Batches with more than N files (e.g., >5 files)

## Root cause

The review-complexity-classifier.sh scores each factor independently but does not have a floor rule for merge commits or multi-file batches. A merge with small per-file diffs can score 0 across all factors and land in light tier, even though the aggregate change is complex.

## Fix needed

1. Add a floor rule in review-complexity-classifier.sh that forces minimum standard tier for merge commits (MERGE_HEAD present)
2. Add a floor rule that forces minimum standard tier when the diff touches more than N source files
3. Document these floor rules in classifier-tier-output.md

comments.body: INTERACTIVITY_DEFERRED: safeguard_approval | LLM-behavioral: agents use haiku for complex reviews. Fix requires editing review workflow (safeguard). Re-run in interactive session to approve.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
